ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 5.6% of revenue ($311,880 on $5,597,043)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

keyConcerns.headline: Expenses are 1.7x revenue ($9,519,616 vs $5,597,043)
keyConcerns.detail: Spending significantly exceeding revenue is unsustainable without reserves drawdown.

score_details.score_summary: The Intercept Media Inc shows good impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://connexions.org/CxLibrary/CX16565.htm)

all_citations.claim: The organization maintains a program expense ratio of 71.7% and holds 8.3 months of working capital.
The Intercept Media Inc. reported a total revenue of $5,597,043 and a fundraising efficiency of $0.26 per $1 raised.
The organization focuses on investigative journalism, podcasts, and documentary filmmaking to expose corruption.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The Intercept earns a moderate score reflecting its solid financial health and operational efficiency, balanced against a lower alignment for donors seeking direct-service or faith-based initiatives.
areas_for_improvement: Limited alignment with traditional Muslim donor priorities due to its focus on general advocacy and media.
Impact measurement is based on capacity building rather than direct service delivery.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is considered medium-low as the mission focuses on general advocacy and journalism rather than specific community-based needs [3]. While it provides a platform for sadaqah-eligible giving, its focus on systemic institutional critique may not fit the specific philanthropic goals of all donors [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates moderate impact through capacity building in the media sector, maintaining a program expense ratio of 71.7% and 8.3 months of working capital [1][2]. While it effectively produces investigative content, the direct social impact per dollar is less quantifiable than direct-aid organizations [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.
strengths: Maintains a program expense ratio of 71.7%, ensuring the majority of funds support journalism and filmmaking.
Strong liquidity with 8.3 months of working capital available to sustain operations.
Efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.26 per $1 raised.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. is a non-profit news organization dedicated to investigative journalism, podcasting, and documentary filmmaking through its Field of Vision program [1][3]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $5,597,043 to support its mission of holding powerful individuals and institutions accountable [2].

With only <cite id="12">4 board members</cite>, the governance structure is considered minimal for an organization of this scale.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is not currently available for this organization.

risk_factors: <cite id="12">Minimal board oversight with only 4 members</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="6">moderate program spending</cite> and a <cite id="12">small governing board</cite>.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and transparency data available.

alignment.explanation: Alignment is lower for traditional donors as the focus is on <cite id="13">general advocacy and journalism</cite> rather than direct humanitarian aid.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="14">clearly stating its sadaqah-eligible status</cite> or highlighting its coverage of issues affecting Muslim communities.

credibility.explanation: The Intercept is highly credible due to its <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and clear <cite id="3">mission of institutional accountability</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="12">expand its board of directors</cite> beyond the current four members to ensure more diverse oversight.

impact.explanation: Impact is primarily driven by <cite id="1">capacity building in the media sector</cite>, with a <cite id="6">71.7% program expense ratio</cite>.
impact.improvement: The organization should <cite id="11">seek external evaluation</cite> or track outcomes over at least three years to better assess the real-world effects of its reporting.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Measured outcomes via Candid

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports independent journalism and media startups

rich_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value journalism as a tool for social justice and want to support investigative efforts that hold global institutions accountable through sadaqah.
donor_motivations: Believe in the power of the press to expose corruption
Value transparency and whistleblowing in government and corporate sectors
Want to support high-quality documentary filmmaking on social issues
Prefer funding systemic change over direct service delivery
giving_considerations: Impact is measured through influence and policy shifts rather than individual beneficiaries
The organization operates in a high-risk legal environment due to its investigative nature
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prefer funding immediate humanitarian needs like food or medical aid.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that while the organization <cite id="11">tracks outcome metrics</cite>, it lacks rigorous external evaluations of its long-term societal impact.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Intercept creates impact by investigating power, which informs the public and acts as a catalyst for civic action and institutional reform.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in journalism helps donors understand how specific reporting leads to tangible policy changes or legal accountability.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a media organization, additional funding allows for more deep-dive investigations and expanded international coverage.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Global coverage including USA and Middle East

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">Field of Vision documentary program</cite> and focus on whistleblowing.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Recipient of Intercept grants for independent reporting
similar_organizations.name: Drop Site News Inc

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a lean fundraising model, spending only <cite id="9">$0.26 per $1 raised</cite>.
The Intercept maintains <cite id="8">8.3 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring operational stability for long-term investigations.
The charity has earned a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: The Intercept's operational model is built on financial sustainability and transparency. By maintaining <cite id="8">8.3 months of operating reserves</cite>, the organization protects its editorial independence from sudden funding fluctuations. Furthermore, its <cite id="10">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite> indicates a commitment to public accountability, providing donors with comprehensive data on its mission and results. This is supported by a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>, which, while lower than some direct-service charities, reflects the high fixed costs of <cite id="1">investigative journalism and documentary filmmaking</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. is a non-profit news organization dedicated to <cite id="1">investigative journalism, podcasting, and documentary filmmaking</cite> through its Field of Vision program. Founded in <cite id="2">2014</cite>, the organization serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing deep-dive reporting to <cite id="3">expose corruption and institutional injustice</cite> across the globe. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="4">total revenue of $18,961,623</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="5">$18,747,165 in contributions</cite> from the public. The Intercept maintains a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>, directing the majority of its resources toward its core editorial and production missions. Beyond its domestic focus, the organization covers critical international issues in regions including <cite id="7">Syria, Iran, Mexico, and Ecuador</cite>. Its financial health is characterized by <cite id="8">8.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for its investigative operations. The organization also demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="9">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable support.
